question_text: A spring is stretched beyond its elastic limit and then released. Which statement describes what happens to the spring?

options.label: A
options.text: The spring returns to its original length because all springs are elastic.

options.label: B
options.text: The spring does not return to its original length because it has been permanently deformed.

options.label: C
options.text: The spring shrinks to a shorter length than it was originally.

options.label: D
options.text: The spring returns to its original length, but only after several hours.

explanation: Once a spring is stretched beyond its elastic limit, the material is permanently damaged and cannot return to its original shape. The elastic limit is the point where a spring stops being able to recover fully. Options A, C, and D all incorrectly suggest the spring will either recover or behave in ways that don't match what actually happens beyond the elastic limit.
